column5: Taliban government vs. ISIS-Khorasan Province + National Resistance Front. ISIS-K conducting attacks. Taliban consolidated control but face economic collapse and international isolation.
ISIS-linked insurgents vs. Mozambican forces + Rwandan troops + SADC force. LNG projects disrupted. Insurgency contained but not defeated.

Boko Haram/ISWAP (northeast), banditry (northwest), separatist tensions (southeast/IPOB), farmer-herder conflicts (Middle Belt). ACLED notes no respite from Nigeria's complex patchwork of regional conflicts.csis
ISIS remnants, Iran-backed Shia militias (PMF/PMU), Kurdish tensions. Low-intensity but persistent. Iran war has re-activated militia operations against US targets.

Seleka/Anti-Balaka cycle. Russian Africa Corps present. Government controls capital; armed groups control much of countryside.
